compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Summary

No CI workflow enables pip dependency caching, so every job/matrix cell
re-downloads and rebuilds the heavy `.[dev]`→`[agents]` stack
(langchain/langgraph/anthropic/pydantic-ai/inspect-ai/opentelemetry). Measured
cold-vs-warm install into a fresh venv with an isolated pip cache: cold ~19s →
warm ~9s locally (cache ~52 MB) — caching roughly halves install time by
avoiding the re-download. The per-job saving is modest but multiplies across the
~10+ jobs/cells that install deps.

## What

Add `cache: 'pip'` + `cache-dependency-path: pyproject.toml` to EVERY
`actions/setup-python` step that subsequently installs project deps, across ALL
`.github/workflows/*`. Deps live in `pyproject.toml` (no separate lockfile), so
the cache is keyed on it: a dependency bump changes the key and invalidates the
cache automatically. Caching is purely additive — a cache miss behaves exactly
as today.

## Scope (files)

Every workflow with a dep-installing `setup-python` step:

- `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml` (require-ticket, build-and-test, golden-path)
- `.github/workflows/_optionality.yml` (reusable; 4 steps)
- `.github/workflows/test.yml` (lint, pip-audit, test, external, golden-path)
- `.github/workflows/eval.yml`, `.github/workflows/eval-plan-review-container.yml`
- `.github/workflows/external-integration.yml`
- `.github/workflows/reconcile-bridge.yml`, `.github/workflows/reconcile-bridge-canary.yml`
- `.github/workflows/mirror-guard.yml`, `.github/workflows/verify-identity.yaml`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `cache: 'pip'` + `cache-dependency-path: pyproject.toml` is set on EVERY
  `actions/setup-python` step that installs project deps across all
  `.github/workflows/*` — verifiable by grep: no dep-installing `setup-python`
  step lacks the two `cache*` inputs.
- [ ] The cache is keyed on `pyproject.toml` content (via
  `cache-dependency-path`), so a dependency change mints a new cache key —
  verifiable from the config alone (no lockfile involved).
- [ ] YAML remains valid (`python -c "import yaml; ..."`) and each affected
  install step is otherwise byte-for-byte unchanged apart from the two added
  `cache*` inputs on its `setup-python`.
- [ ] [operator-attested] A repeat CI run on an unchanged `pyproject.toml` logs a
  pip cache hit (the `setup-python` "restore cache" step reports a cache
  restore), recorded as a comment citing the specific run.
- [ ] Change passes CI (`Verified +1`) and lands via Gerrit.
compiled_state.title: Enable pip caching on all setup-python steps in CI workflows
